economic_notes: First Nation is undertaking major capital projects including a new water treatment plant (construction in progress $15,191,566) and bridge construction. COVID-19 caused lockdowns, travel restrictions, and slowed capital project progress. Portfolio investment of $255,000 in 2317186 Ontario Limited o/a Happy Time Tours and Travel (15% ownership). Neskantaga Economic Development Corporation is a consolidated subsidiary.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to incomplete tangible capital asset inventory — the First Nation has not fully adopted PSAB Section 3150 and cannot confirm all major asset classes are recognized. Net debt position of $11,023,898. Large deferred revenue balance of $10,559,839 primarily from ISC ($10,274,726), driven by COVID-19 funding flexibility allowing deferral to future years. Capital projects segment shows large surplus ($2,764,046) due to timing of project expenditures vs. funding received. No schedule of remuneration was included in the financial statements provided. Expenses_infrastructure represents Community Infrastructure segment. Capital Projects segment expenses ($1,481,427) not mapped to a standard category. Annual debt service reflects repayments only; new advances of $403,278 also occurred.
